_comment: BRING YOUR OWN CORPUS. This is a tiny SYNTHETIC ground-truth fixture that exercises the harness end-to-end (see ../README.md). It is NOT a real corpus and must NOT be cited as a recall number. Each entry is one KNOWN vulnerability the scanner is expected to surface; recall = the fraction it does. Point the runner at your own file with --corpus <path.json>. Schema per entry: { finding_id, repo, commit, type, location, root_cause, description }. The runner also accepts a bare top-level array of such entries.

entries.root_cause: user-controlled `req.query.name` concatenated directly into a SQL string passed to db.query
entries.description: Login lookup builds its WHERE clause by string concatenation of an unvalidated request parameter.

entries.root_cause: user input interpolated into a shell string handed to child_process.exec
entries.description: The report-export endpoint shells out to a CLI with an attacker-controlled filename.

entries.root_cause: request path joined to a base dir without normalization, allowing ../ escape
entries.description: The file-download handler reads an arbitrary path derived from the request without canonicalization.

entries.root_cause: server fetches a fully user-supplied URL with no allow-list, enabling internal-network access
entries.description: A link-preview feature fetches any URL the user provides, reachable to cloud metadata endpoints.
